_instructions.slide_naming: slide-01.jpg, slide-02.jpg ... (exported from PowerPoint → File → Export → JPEG/PNG)
_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Smart Classroom Alert System
subtitle: Monitor Classroom Temperature Automatically!
overview: Students build a Smart Classroom Alert System using a DHT temperature sensor, LCD display, and buzzer. The system continuously monitors room temperature. If the temperature rises above 38°C, the LCD displays 'Too Hot!' and the buzzer sounds an alert. This project introduces environmental monitoring and smart automation concepts.

images._note: Add photos of DHT sensor, LCD display, buzzer connections, classroom monitoring setup, and student activities.

youtubeVideos.title: DHT11 Sensor with Arduino
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to measure temperature using a DHT11 sensor.

youtubeVideos.title: LCD Display with Arduino
youtubeVideos.desc: Display sensor readings on a 16x2 LCD.

youtubeVideos.title: Arduino Temperature Alarm Project
youtubeVideos.desc: Create a temperature-based warning system using Arduino.

activity.title: Build a Smart Temperature Alert System
steps: Connect the DHT11 sensor to Arduino
Connect the buzzer to a digital pin
Display current temperature on the LCD
Increase the sensor temperature using your hand or warm air
Discuss real-world applications of environmental monitoring

quiz.q: Which sensor is used to measure temperature in this project?
quiz.explanation: The DHT11 sensor measures temperature and humidity.

quiz.q: What happens when the temperature exceeds 38°C?
options: LCD displays 'Too Hot!' and buzzer sounds
quiz.explanation: The system alerts users with a message and buzzer sound.
The LCD shows temperature readings and warning messages.

quiz.q: What is the alert temperature in this project?
quiz.explanation: The alert is triggered when temperature exceeds 38°C.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of the buzzer?
quiz.explanation: The buzzer warns users when temperature becomes too high.

quiz.q: Which device controls the entire system?
quiz.explanation: Arduino UNO reads sensor data and controls outputs.

quiz.q: Which programming concept is used to check if temperature is above 38°C?
quiz.explanation: An if statement is used to compare temperature values.
DHT sensors measure humidity and temperature digitally.

quiz.q: This project is an example of:
quiz.explanation: The project monitors environmental conditions in a classroom.

quiz.q: Where can similar systems be used?
quiz.explanation: Temperature monitoring systems are useful in many places.
